content: TME characterization guides treatment selection. Gene expression profiling: immunoscore quantifies CD3+ and CD8+ T-cell density in tumor core and invasive margin (validated in colorectal cancer); T-cell inflamed gene signature predicts checkpoint inhibitor response; TGF-β signature identifies patients unlikely to benefit from immunotherapy alone. Multiplex immunohistochemistry: simultaneous detection of multiple markers (CD3, CD8, PD-1, PD-L1, FOXP3, CD68) preserves spatial relationships; identifies immune cell localization and interactions. Spatial transcriptomics: maps gene expression to tissue architecture; reveals cell-cell communication networks. Imaging biomarkers: PET tracers for immune cell tracking (investigational); radiomics extracts features from CT/MRI correlating with TME characteristics. Circulating biomarkers: cytokine profiles; exosome content; ctDNA dynamics reflect TME changes. Functional assays: organoid co-cultures with autologous immune cells test drug sensitivity. TME assessment increasingly integrated into clinical trials for patient stratification.
